critic.verdict: An exceptionally fluid and satisfying Easy chart with perfect foot alternation and excellent vocal synchronization, marred only by a brief timing and quantization glitch around the 81-second mark.

issues.problem: The HOLD L ending at 81.391 and the HOLD U starting at 81.483 and ending at 82.037 are off-grid and unquantized. This creates a very tight, awkward 92ms gap between the holds and results in off-beat releases that are difficult for beginners to judge.

critic.verdict: An exceptionally fluid and rhythmically engaging chart that captures the house track's syncopated energy with great physical flow.

issues.problem: The transition from JUMP LR directly into an eighth-note tap U (41.783) is quite fast and can feel slightly spikey in difficulty for a medium-tier chart.
A rapid eighth-note transition from JUMP UR to tap L (43.629) requires very quick foot recovery, slightly exceeding the standard physical pacing of the rest of the layout.

critic.verdict: An energetic and rhythmically accurate chart that maps beautifully to the vocal hooks, let down by a couple of physically awkward hold-tap combinations.

issues.problem: Creates a 3-panel hold (U, D, and L active simultaneously from 59.094s to 59.329s) on a singles chart, forcing an awkward hand/knee drop or bracket that doesn't fit the standard flow of the chart.

While holding U, the player must tap L at 81.137s and R at 81.252s. This requires a 16th-note one-foot slide/switch between L and R at 130 BPM, which is extremely difficult and uncomfortable to execute physically.
The 16th-note run U -> R -> D -> U forces either a rapid body spin or a highly awkward double-step on the Up panel, disrupting the otherwise smooth flow of the section.
